business.headline: EPF Scheme 2026: What employees and employers need to knowWith around eight crore active EPFO subscribers, the government hopes the updated scheme will make provident fund management simpler, more efficient and better suited to a digital-first workplace.

Will petrol, diesel prices be cut? Hardeep Puri says here's when it's possiblePuri explained that although international crude oil prices have fallen in recent weeks, oil companies are still refining crude that was purchased when prices were much higher during the West Asia conflict.

Centre eyes LIC, PSU stake sales to cushion strain from oil crisis: ReportThe Centre is speeding up stake sales in LIC, Hindustan Zinc and other state-run firms. The move is aimed at creating fiscal room as oil price volatility pressures government finances.

Sensex jumps 579 points as IT stocks shine; Nifty closes above 24,175The BSE Sensex climbed 579.48 points, or 0.75%, to close at 77,502.12, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 169.85 points, or 0.71%, to settle at 24,175.70.

AI rally cracks: Why Asian markets are crashing while Indian IT stocks rallyAsian chip stocks slumped after reports stoked fears of excess AI computing capacity. Indian IT shares, more tied to software spending, rose as investors weighed a different AI demand story.

Want to save more tax under new regime? Ask your employer about this NPS benefitWith ITR filing in full swing, our 'KNOW YOUR TAX' series helps you understand the tax benefits that still matter. This edition explains how an employer's NPS contribution can help salaried employees save tax while building a retirement corpus.
Mehli Mistry steps down from Ratan Tata's family officeMistry's resignation from RNT Associates comes months after he was removed from Tata Trusts in November 2025.

EPFO new rule: PF contribution above Rs 1,800 now voluntary; what it means for youThe updated rules are expected to make the EPF system simpler, more digital and easier to use for nearly 8 crore active EPFO subscribers. The new scheme came into effect on June 29 after it was published in the Gazette, replacing the long-standing EPF Scheme, 1952.

Why are IT stocks rising today? Infosys, HCLTech, TCS jump up to 5%Infosys, HCLTech, TCS rise: IT stocks rebounded sharply on Thursday, led by Infosys and other large-cap names. The recovery reflected a shift from overheated AI chip plays to software firms seen as steadier AI beneficiaries.

Rs 35,000-crore investment push linked to Maruti plant during Japan PM's visitThe Kharkhoda plant is Maruti Suzuki's fourth manufacturing facility in India and is expected to significantly boost the country's passenger vehicle production capacity.

Sensex jumps nearly 400 points, Nifty tops 24,100 as IT stocks reboundIT stocks led the rally, while falling crude oil prices and easing geopolitical concerns boosted investor confidence. However, a weak monsoon remains a key concern.

OTT subscriptions becoming a trap? 8 in 10 users say they're being manipulatedA nationwide survey has found that eight in ten OTT users in India say they have encountered 'dark patterns', deceptive design practices that nudge users into spending more, continuing subscriptions or making choices they may not have intended.

Energy crisis: Is the worst now over? Sajjid Chinoy respondsPrices of commercial LPG were cut by Rs 183.50 per 19-kg cylinder on Wednesday – the first reduction this year following a series of hikes in the wake of the West Asia crisis that caused energy supply disruptions across the globe.

Red Bull, PepsiCo, Sting among 6 energy drink brands get FSSAI noticesThe FSSAI issued notices to six energy drink brands -- Red Bull Energy Drink, PepsiCo's Adrenaline Rush Energy Drink, Reliance Consumer Products' Campa Energy Drink Gold Boost, Sting Energy Drink, Hell Energy and Coca-Cola-backed Monster Energy -- over misbranding and misleading claims.

PPF, NSC, SCSS interest rates for July-September 2026 declared: Has anything changed?The Senior Citizen Savings Scheme (SCSS) and Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana (SSY) will continue to offer 8.2%, the highest among the major small savings schemes.

Indian women are taking charge of their money. Here's how they are investingThe image of women as only careful savers is fast changing. Today, more Indian women are investing, exploring digital platforms and taking control of their financial future.

EPFO extends online service outage till July 2: What does it mean for members?EPAs part of the upgrade, members will not be able to submit fresh claims through the EPFO portal during the maintenance period. The organisation has also temporarily stopped the processing of claims while the migration work is underway.

Sensex rises over 440 points, Nifty closes above 24,000; IT stocks cap gainsFinancial, auto and FMCG stocks powered the rally, while weakness in IT shares and concerns over a weak monsoon kept investors cautious.

RBI's latest worry isn't inflation. It's artificial intelligenceArtificial intelligence is no longer just transforming banking—it is emerging as one of the biggest threats to the financial system. In its latest Financial Stability Report, the RBI has warned that AI-powered cyberattacks, dependence on common AI platforms and growing technology risks could pose fresh challenges for banks and financial institutions.

IndiGo's new budget-friendly 'Lite' fare for cabin baggage flyers takes off July 15According to the airline, the fare is designed for passengers travelling with only cabin baggage. By paying only for the services they use, customers can have greater control over their travel costs.

entertainment.headline: Lock Upp 2: Vinny Arora calls Shreya Kalra a loser for targeting husband Dheeraj Actor Vinny Arora has hit back at social media influencer Shreya Kalra after her on-screen clash with Dheeraj Dhoopar in Lock Upp: Sach Ya Sazaa. Vinny accused Shreya for her "cheap, revenge-seeking" strategy against Dheeraj.

SS Rajamouli joins Martin Scorsese, Clint Eastwood on France's Wall of Filmmakers Director SS Rajamouli was honoured at Institut Lumière in Lyon with a plaque on its Wall of Filmmakers. The recognition places him alongside Hollywood icons like Martin Scorsese and Clint Eastwood and adds to the global reach of his work.

Vicky Kaushal's three-word monsoon post for Katrina Kaif is pure romance Actor Vicky Kaushal shared a rain-soaked black-and-white photograph of wife Katrina Kaif hugging him from behind. The post drew fan reactions and spotlighted the couple's life after welcoming their son Vihaan.

Homebound cinematographer Pratik Shah apologises over sexual misconduct allegations Homebound cinematographer Pratik Shah has apologised for online allegations of sexual misconduct and emotional abuse made last year. His statement follows months of fallout, including reported removals from projects and final credits.

Jailer 2 release date announced: Rajinikanth's action sequel to arrive in October Rajinikanth's Jailer 2 finally has a release date. Sun Pictures has unveiled when the much-awaited sequel will arrive in cinemas, along with fresh details about the returning team and expanded cast.

Aamir Khan-Gauri Spratt to skip grand reception after Sunday wedding: Source Aamir Khan and Gauri Spratt are set to marry at the actor's home on Sunday. The couple plan no grand reception, opting instead for a private family celebration after the ceremony.

Raavan intern to Academy invite: Costume designer Eka Lakhani reflects on her journey Fresh off her Academy invitation, costume designer Eka Lakhani speaks to India Today about the surprise honour, her journey in cinema, Bhanu Athaiya's legacy, and why Indian craftsmanship is finally earning global recognition.

Sanjay Gupta compares Siya Goyal to Rhea Chakraborty, and internet is not having it Filmmaker Sanjay Gupta urged people not to put Siya Goyal through a media trial before the evidence is tested. His comparison with Rhea Chakraborty's experience drew a sharp online backlash as police investigated Ketan Agarwal's death as alleged murder.

Harshad Chopda recalls running away from gay man as Sufi discusses sexuality A discussion on sexuality and identity in Lock Upp episode five prompted candid accounts from Sufi Motiwala, Dheeraj Dhoopar and Harshad Chopda. The exchange highlighted fear, family rejection and the stereotypes queer people continue to face.

Alpha box office Day 1 prediction: Alia Bhatt's film set for Rs 7-8 crore opening Actor Alia Bhatt and Sharvari's Alpha is set for a theatrical release on July 3. The female-led spy thriller is set for a Rs 7-8 crore opening, according to the early advance booking trends.

Isakapatnam review: Aishwarya Rajesh's crime drama is familiar, flawed but engagingRelease Date:Thu Jul 02, 2026 Isakapatnam review: Set against the backdrop of a coastal town, Isakapatnam is a grounded crime drama that thrives on power struggles, shifting loyalties and strong performances. It may not reinvent the genre, but it tells a familiar story with enough conviction to keep you hooked till the end.

Richa Chadha tears into Dia Mirza trolls, says critics can 'bully a 5-year-old' Richa Chadha defended Dia Mirza after a podcast clip about her son Avyaan triggered online trolling. Her response shifted attention to the backlash, the criticism of a five-year-old, and Dia's environmental advocacy.

Why Taylor Swift-Travis Kelce's New York wedding is America's royal moment Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's reported New York wedding is shaping up to be America's biggest celebrity event. From the rumoured Madison Square Garden venue to the star-studded guest list, here's everything we know.

Stebin Ben weighs in on Kriti Sanon's wedding speculation and Kabir Bahia rumours Stebin Ben has addressed the ongoing speculation around Kriti Sanon's marriage amid dating rumours with Kabir Bahia. His comments offer a fresh perspective on the actor's personal life and why there should be no rush.

The new face of female action in South cinema: Samantha, Priety and Abhirami What makes Maa Inti Bangaaram and Blast stand out isn't that women are performing the action, but that they do so without sacrificing character, emotion or believability.

Project Hail Mary OTT release: When and where to watch Ryan Gosling's sci-fi film Amazon MGM Studios has announced that Project Hail Mary will premiere on Prime Video on 3 July and released a new clip. The Ryan Gosling-led adaptation follows Ryland Grace as he tries to solve the mystery behind the dying sun, with an unexpected friendship reshaping the mission.

Jana Nayagan leak case: 1.2 crore watched Vijay film illegally, police tells court Chennai police told the Madras High Court that a leaked copy of Vijay's Jana Nayagan was watched by 1.2 crore people before certification and release. The submission came as the court rejected bail for two accused in the Jana Nayagan leak case.

Director Abhinay Deo on his films called ahead of time: Never understood the label Director Abhinay Deo said he never understood why some of his films are labelled "ahead of their time." In an exclusive chat with India Today, he also recalled convincing Karisma Kapoor to star in Brown and shared an update on a possible 24 revival.

Actor Jean Paul Lal on Balan success: It's fantastic, especially as a nepo kid Actor Jean Paul Lal has spoken about the success of Balan The Boy and his father Lal's emotional response to the film. He said the appreciation feels amazing, especially as a nepo kid.

general news.headline: Mojtaba Khamenei won't attend father's funeral: Iran Supreme Leader's aideIn an exclusive interview with India Today, Ayatollah Hakim Elahi said the decision was driven by security concerns as tensions with Israel remain high ahead of the state funeral.

After WhatsApp, govt sends notice to Telegram, Signal over username featuresThe government has sought explanations from Telegram and Signal on how they are addressing risks related to fraud, impersonation and other forms of online abuse associated with usernames.

Got 38 parasites in brain after India visit, says UK woman. 3 facts and a questionA British woman claims that a 2007 trip to India left her with 38 parasites in her brain after she contracted neurocysticercosis, a rare disease caused by the pork tapeworm. Here are three key facts about how the infection spreads, and whether her India visit can really be identified as the source.

ESP 3–0 AUT Live: Spain too hot to handle for AustriaSpain (ESP) vs Austria (AUT) Live, FIFA World Cup: (ESP 3–0 AUT) Spain underlined their title credentials with a dominant 3-0 win over Austria, powered by Mikel Oyarzabal's brace and Pedro Porro's maiden international goal. Luis de la Fuente's side barely looked troubled as they booked their place in the World Cup Round of 16 in emphatic fashion.

IITian chooses peace over pay, rejects Rs 32 LPA Gurgaon job to stay in BengaluruAn IIT Roorkee graduate and data scientist has gone viral after rejecting a Rs 32 LPA job offer in Gurgaon to stay in Bengaluru. Citing safety, peace of mind and quality of life, her decision has sparked a wider debate on what truly defines a successful career.

125-year-old gurdwara demolished in Pakistan; distressing, says IndiaIndia has condemned the demolition of a historic Sikh shrine in Pakistan as a deeply distressing act of vandalism. Sikh organisations and political leaders have demanded accountability and restoration of the gurdwara, and stronger protection for religious minorities.

Pak Army gave guns to Kashmiris, call us terrorists: POK leader as protests spiralPOK protest leader Sardar Aman Khan accused Islamabad of hypocrisy for labelling activists as terrorists, as anti-government protests rage in the region.

What is SSPX, the secretive rebel Catholic group expelled by the PopeThe SSPX, a group of ultra-conservative Catholics, consecrated four bishops and livestreamed the ceremony in defiance of Pope Leo. The Vatican was quick to expel the members. The incident has revived scrutiny of the traditionalist group's long conflict with the Church.

Wildfires sweep southern France as European heatwave fuel thousands of blazesWildfires swept across southern France, stretching crews in Aude, Herault and near Marseille. Heat, drought and strong winds are worsening risks as no rain is forecast.

ENG vs SA semifinals Highlights: England qualify for final, beat South Africa by 40 runsEngland (ENG-W) vs South Africa (SA-W) semifinals Highlights, Women's T20 World Cup: England have stormed to the final of the tournament after beating South Africa by 40 runs at the Oval. They will now face Australia in the final on July 5.

Men hack e-rickshaws with Bluetooth-based app, then charge drivers to fix themPolice in Ujjain have detained an 18-year-old suspect in a case involving e-rickshaws allegedly disrupted through a mobile app. The probe now centres on technical evidence, CCTV footage and warnings to drivers against paying unknown helpers.

Day 4 Highlights: Alexander Zverev beats Valentin Royer, Elena Rybakina into Round 3Wimbledon 2026 live score: Defending champions Iga Switak has beaten Karolina Pliskova to advance to the third round. Sixth seed Taylor Fritz has won his men's singles second-round match while Alexander Zverev has gone through to the third round.

Watch: At PM Modi's request, Japanese PM plays santoor at Delhi cultural eventJapanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi embraced India's rich cultural heritage by trying her hand at the santoor during a cultural luncheon hosted by Prime Minister Narendra Modi in New Delhi.

8th Pay Commission: From Rs 18,000 to Rs 69,000? Minimum pay debate explainedEmployee organisations believe the current minimum basic pay of Rs 18,000, introduced nearly a decade ago under the 7th Pay Commission, no longer reflects the cost of living. Depending on the method used, unions have proposed increasing it to anywhere between Rs 55,000 and nearly Rs 69,000.

2 pregnant women die, Navi Mumbai hospital under fire over negligence claimsTwo pregnant women have died and another is battling for life amid allegations of medical negligence at a Navi Mumbai civic hospital.

PM Modi calls Japan PM his younger sister, she responds with a smileFor a summit centred on diplomacy and strategic cooperation, the most memorable moment came when PM Modi called his Japanese counterpart, Sanae Takaichi, his "younger sister", and she responded by saying the two leaders would take the India-Japan relationship forward with the trust and warmth of "brother and sister."

First he cheats, then calls: Old video shows Siya abusing on phone while partyingAn old video has surfaced showing Siya Goyal drinking at a pub and using abusive language during a heated phone call, in which she is heard saying, "First he cheats on me, then he calls me."

600 Hammer bombs, anti-tank missiles among Rs 5,000-crore deal set for nodThe Defence Ministry is likely to clear major procurement proposals worth thousands of crores, including Hammer precision-guided munitions, Verba air defence systems, indigenous MP-ATGMs and high-altitude pseudo satellites.

Pak uses cement to conserve Vedic-era Taxila, gets Unesco delisting warningThe UN's cultural arm, Unesco, has warned Pakistan over its "conservation work" in the Vedic-era archeological complex of Taxila. Unesco said that Pakistan's use of cement in the "conservation" of the ancient sites is a violation of restoration norms and could result in the site being delisted from its list of World Heritage Sites.

CRPF jawan shoots himself dead at judge's residence in DelhiA CRPF jawan posted at a judge's residence in Delhi allegedly shot himself while on duty. Police have begun an inquiry, with no foul play suspected at this stage.

Chetan Bhagat's viral 'regressive baniya family' theory behind Siya-Ketan caseIn an interview with India Today, Chetan Bhagat said family shame lay at the centre of the Pune murder case. The case, which involved the gruesome murder of businessman Ketan Agarwal by his fiancee Siya and her boyfriend, has whipped up a massive media frenzy.

How Vijay govt is saving big for Tamil Nadu, and contractors are thanking himTamil Nadu Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay-led government's decision to open even the smallest public works projects for civic tender has resulted in contractors quoting sharply lower rates, resulting in big gains for the state's exchequer.

Class 12 board exam marks may get 50% weightage in NEET, JEE-based admissionsSources said board examination marks may be given 50 per cent weightage in NEET and JEE-linked admissions. The proposal is part of a wider review of exam fairness, coaching dependence and testing reforms.

The new scheme came into effect on June 29 after it was published in the Gazette, replacing the long-standing EPF Scheme, 1952.

sports.headline: England Women bury South Africa ghosts, set up blockbuster final vs Australia Women's T20 World Cup, ENGW vs SAW: England beat South Africa by 40 runs and set up a final with Australia on Sunday at the iconic Lord's Cricket Ground in London.

Elena Rybakina sends World No.1 warning, but refuses to get carried away Elena Rybakina played down the World No. 1 race, saying her focus is solely on improving and winning matches at Wimbledon. The Australian Open champion could overtake Aryna Sabalenka with a deep run at the All England Club.

Alexander Zverev, Elena Rybakina ease into Round 3 with clinical displays French Open champion Alexander Zverev and former Wimbledon winner Elena Rybakina sent out strong title statements with dominant second-round wins. Both stars advanced in straight sets, showcasing the form that makes them leading contenders at the All England Club.

Iga Swiatek braces for tricky grass test against high-flying Alexandra Eala Defending champion Iga Swiatek is expecting a stern third-round test against Alexandra Eala at Wimbledon, with their head-to-head locked at 1-1 and the Pole describing the Filipina's grass-court game as tricky.

ESP 3–0 AUT Live: Spain too hot to handle for Austria Spain (ESP) vs Austria (AUT) Live, FIFA World Cup: (ESP 3–0 AUT) Spain underlined their title credentials with a dominant 3-0 win over Austria, powered by Mikel Oyarzabal's brace and Pedro Porro's maiden international goal. Luis de la Fuente's side barely looked troubled as they booked their place in the World Cup Round of 16 in emphatic fashion.

Can Lewis Hamilton stop Mercedes? His cheeky answer Is going viral Lewis Hamilton joked that only loosening Mercedes bolts would make him a title threat before Silverstone. The remark exposed Ferrari's gap to the benchmark as Kimi Antonelli's rookie campaign continues to dictate the season.

ENG vs SA semifinals Highlights: England qualify for final, beat South Africa by 40 runs England (ENG-W) vs South Africa (SA-W) semifinals Highlights, Women's T20 World Cup: England have stormed to the final of the tournament after beating South Africa by 40 runs at the Oval. They will now face Australia in the final on July 5.

Turkey's Zeynep Sonmez steals spotlight with unique symbol on racket Turkish player Zeynep Sonmez used a watermelon-shaped racket dampener at Wimbledon 2026 after reportedly being barred from wearing a Palestine-support brooch, saying the fruit symbol allowed her to express solidarity with Palestinians.

India's football capital, Kolkata dresses up for World Cup knockouts FIFA World Cup 2026: A neighbourhood club in Kolkata has turned a Shyambazar lane into a World Cup-themed FIFA Gali with murals, flags and giant cutouts of football stars. The display captures the city's enduring football obsession and its hope that India will one day reach the FIFA World Cup.

Jai Moondra rewarded for heroics vs India with European T20 League berth Ireland pacer Jai Moondra was rewarded for his match-winning performances against India by becoming Rotterdam Dockers' first pick in the inaugural European T20 Premier League Draft.

Iga Swiatek cruises through to Round 3, Fritz, De Minaur maintain perfect starts Defending champion Iga Swiatek stormed into the Wimbledon third round with a straight-sets win over Karolina Pliskova, while Taylor Fritz and Alex de Minaur also advanced with commanding victories.

Samit Dravid forges own path in Maharaja Trophy as fans see shades of Rahul DravidSamit Dravid continues to impress in the Maharaja Trophy KSCA T20 2026, with fans drawing comparisons to father Rahul Dravid as the youngster showcases his own promising white-ball game for Kalyani Bengaluru Blasters.

Portugal vs Croatia livestreaming: Can Ronaldo deliver when it matters most?FIFA World Cup 2026: Portugal meet Croatia in Toronto in a high-stakes Round of 32 tie. The knockout contest could mark Cristiano Ronaldo's or Luka Modric's final appearance on football's biggest stage.

Galle, Colombo to host India in August Test series. Sri Lanka announces scheduleSri Lanka Cricket has confirmed Galle and Colombo as the venues for India's two-Test series in August. The matches carry World Test Championship importance for India as tougher assignments follow.

Shah Rukh Khan unveils new Los Angeles cricket stadium, thanks Jay Shah for supportShah Rukh Khan unveiled the Knight Riders Cricket Ground in Los Angeles, thanking Jay Shah and the ICC for support. The venue strengthens Major League Cricket in Southern California before cricket's LA28 Olympic return.

Sooryavanshi would have taken their pants off: Shastri's blunt verdict goes viralRavi Shastri has slammed India's decision to leave Vaibhav Sooryavanshi on the bench, insisting the 15-year-old should have debuted against Ireland and claiming he "would have taken their pants off" on a slow, small ground.

15-year-old made to wait: What exactly is India's plan for Vaibhav Sooryavanshi?India tour of England: Fast-tracked into the senior squad following a historic IPL campaign, the decision to leave 15-year-old prodigy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi on the bench invites a compelling question over India's delicate balance between hierarchy and current merit.

Balogun did not deserve a red card: USA coach questions referee's decisionUnited States coach Mauricio Pochettino launched a passionate defence of Folarin Balogun after the striker's controversial sending-off in the 2-0 World Cup round-of-32 victory over Bosnia and Herzegovina on Wednesday.

Djokovic defends Serena Williams's Wimbledon return: Just enjoy her greatnessNovak Djokovic has urged fans to celebrate Serena Williams's return to Wimbledon rather than rush to criticise her after the 44-year-old's comeback ended in a first-round defeat.

England out to break South Africa curse with Nat Sciver-Brunt boost for semisWomen's T20 World Cup, ENG vs SA: England have lost their last two knockout matches against South Africa and will be eager to snap that streak when the two sides meet in the second semi-final at The Kennington Oval in London on Thursday.

Sooryavanshi's wait for India debut will put him under more pressure: GavaskarBatting great Sunil Gavaskar believes delaying Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's senior India debut will only increase the pressure on the teenage opener, who was once again left out of the playing XI for the opening T20I against England on Wednesday.

10-man USA keep dream alive, reach last 16 with win over Bosnia & HerzegovinaMalik Tillman spared the United States an anxious finish with a sublime late free-kick as the co-hosts defeated Bosnia and Herzegovina 2-0 on Wednesday to reach the World Cup Round of 16, despite playing the final stages with 10 men after Folarin Balogun's dismissal.

Heartbreaking moment as DR Congo coach learns of father's death after World Cup exitMoments after DR Congo's 1-2 defeat to England ended their historic World Cup run, coach Sebastien Desabre was informed of his father's death during the post-match press conference.

Thomas Tuchel urges parents to let children stay up for England's clash with MexicoEngland manager Thomas Tuchel may have slept through Mexico's victory over Ecuador, but after his side's dramatic 2-1 comeback win over DR Congo sealed a place in the World Cup Round of 16, he had a message for parents back home: let the children stay up to watch Sunday's showdown at the Azteca Stadium.

USA 2-0 BIH Highlights: Balogun, Tillman fire USA into Round of 16USA vs Bosnia & Herzegovina (BIH) Highlights, FIFA World Cup: Full-time: The United States are into the Round of 16 after a composed 2-0 victory over Bosnia and Herzegovina in San Francisco. Folarin Balogun broke the deadlock late in the first half before Malik Tillman doubled the advantage after the interval to seal progression. The hosts also overcame Balogun's second-half red card, producing a disciplined defensive display to see out the contest with 10 men and book their place in the next round.

Senegal's night of promise ends in heartbreak as Belgium pull off the great escapeBelgium completed a stunning comeback from 2-0 down to beat Senegal 3-2 after extra time, with Youri Tielemans converting a controversial late VAR-awarded penalty. The Lions of Teranga were left heartbroken after seeing a famous World Cup upset slip away.

Harry Kane on Lionel Messi's level? Gordon makes big GOAT comparison after England winFIFA World Cup 2026: Anthony Gordon hailed Harry Kane after the England captain's brace turned a fraught tie against DR Congo. The comeback win sent England into the last 16 against co-hosts Mexico and underlined Kane's authority.

Novak Djokovic pranks ball girl, then outclasses Stefanos Tsitsipas to reach Round 3Novak Djokovic entertained Centre Court with a playful prank on a ball girl before cruising past Stefanos Tsitsipas 6-3, 6-4, 6-2 to reach the third round of Wimbledon 2026 on Wednesday.

Harry Kane's rocket sinks DR Congo, breaks Pele's World Cup record in styleHarry Kane scored twice as England came from behind to beat DR Congo 2-1 and reach the Round of 16. His late winner took him past Pele's World Cup tally as DR Congo coach Sebastien Desabre hailed him as the world's best striker.

Shreyas Iyer's wait for first win as India captain prolongs after Durham T20I washoutENG vs IND, 1st T20I: Abhishek Sharma's blistering 59 and Shreyas Iyer's fluent 68 powered India to 189 for 7 in the opening T20I against England, but persistent rain prevented the hosts from beginning their chase in Durham.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
